jobs.title: CLS Toxicology Supervisor
jobs.description:
The Supervisor, Analytical Laboratory oversees daily operations and performance of analytical laboratory processes utilizing immunoassay and/or LC-MS/MS technology. This role ensures high-quality testing, regulatory compliance, and effective supervision of laboratory personnel while supporting ongoing process improvements and technical development initiatives.

Responsibilities

Laboratory Operations & Oversight

Provides day-to-day supervision of testing personnel and oversees reporting of laboratory test results for all CLIA-regulated methods.
Maintains required supervisory presence and accessibility through on-site, telephone, or electronic consultation.
Ensures laboratory operations comply with state and federal regulations, including CLIA General Supervisor responsibilities.
Monitors analytical performance, specimen examination, and adherence to established quality standards for immunoassay and/or LC-MS/MS workflows.
Identifies and resolves technical issues, working closely with laboratory leadership as needed.

Quality, Compliance & Documentation

Ensures Laboratory Occurrence Management (LOM) forms and corrective actions are completed accurately.
Participates in internal audits and proficiency testing.
Oversees and assists with preventive maintenance and calibration of laboratory instruments; coordinates with vendors for repairs or troubleshooting.
Develops, writes, and maintains Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and departmental forms.
Ensures adherence to Quality Assurance, HIPAA, confidentiality, safety regulations, and cybersecurity standards.

Staff Leadership & Development

Provides direct supervision and training for Laboratory Technicians and Assistants in accordance with regulatory requirements.
Conducts performance evaluations for direct reports.
Oversees onboarding, competency assessments, and orientation of laboratory personnel.
Supports continuing education requirements and fosters a culture of professional development.

Technical Expertise & Process Improvement

Performs technical studies to validate or improve clinical testing methods.
Collaborates with vendors on instrument validations and new technology implementations.
Assists with advanced troubleshooting involving equipment, instruments, and assay performance.
Participates in initiatives related to quality, safety, workflow efficiency, and document management.

Additional Responsibilities

Ensures prompt implementation of remedial actions when test systems deviate from performance specifications.
Maintains awareness of evolving laboratory needs and supports organizational goals with flexibility.
Performs additional duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Education & Licensure

Bachelor’s degree in chemical, physical, biological, or clinical laboratory science (required).
Current California CLS license (Generalist, Toxicologist Scientist, or Clinical Chemist Scientist).
Out-of-state Medical Technologists may be considered if able to obtain a California CLS license within six months of hire.

Experience

Bachelor’s degree with 3+ years of CLS Leadership experience, or

Master’s degree with 2+ years of CLS Leadership experience, or

Minimum 2 years of experience after obtaining CLS/MT licensure.
Supervisory experience strongly preferred.

Technical & Professional Skills

Knowledgeable in clinical laboratory testing methods, techniques, equipment, and supplies.
Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills at all organizational levels.
High attention to detail and accuracy.
Demonstrated ability to lead staff and manage projects effectively.
Must meet continuing education requirements per California Department of Health Services.

Physical Requirements

Sitting: 50%
Walking: 30%
Standing: 20%
Ability to lift up to 50 lbs
Ability to read/interpret SOPs and convert instructions into daily tasks

Benefits

Medical, Dental, Vision, and Disability Insurance
401(k) with Company Match
Paid Time Off and Holidays
Tuition Assistance
Behavioral and Health Care Resources

Must be a U.S Citizen, not offering sponsorship at this time

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Science

Industries

Biotechnology Research, Hospitals and Health Care, and Hospitals

jobs.title: Environmental Health Safety Specialist
jobs.description:
Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) Specialist

Our client is seeking an experienced Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) Specialist
to support the execution of utility-scale energy storage projects and ensure compliance with environmental, health, and safety regulations. This role will focus on risk management, employee safety, incident prevention, and continuous improvement across project operations.

Key Responsibilities

Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local EHS regulations
Conduct safety audits, inspections, and risk assessments
Develop and deliver EHS training programs
Investigate incidents, accidents, and near-misses
Support emergency response planning and preparedness
Manage environmental compliance, waste handling, and sustainability initiatives
Maintain EHS documentation, records, and regulatory reporting
Drive continuous improvement in workplace safety and compliance

Requirements

5+ years of EHS experience in industrial, manufacturing, construction, energy, or related environments
Strong knowledge of OSHA and environmental compliance standards
Experience with risk assessments, incident investigations, and safety programs
Excellent communication, training, and organizational skills
Ability to work independently and collaboratively across teams

Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Environmental Science, Safety Management, Public Health, or related field
Certifications such as CSP, CIH, CHMM, OSHA Outreach Trainer, or NEBOSH

Experience supporting large-scale energy, industrial, manufacturing, or construction projects
Experience implementing EHS management systems and improving safety performance metrics

Location & Schedule

Fresno County, CA

On-site
Full-Time

Compensation

Competitive hourly rate + benefits

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Associate

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Management and Manufacturing

Industries

Renewable Energy Semiconductor Manufacturing
